jobDistinction.legacyPausedAuditJob: Broad website issue discovery plus small automatic fixes/upgrades; currently paused.
jobDistinction.currentRemediationJob: Backlog-driven remediation, optimization, and upgrade loop based on .hermes/remediation-backlog.md.

nonOverlapPolicy: If the previous remediation run is still active, skip the current tick instead of starting another fix run.

freshAuditPolicy.action: Run comprehensive audit, update remediation-backlog.md with newly discovered or reprioritized issues, then continue remediation.

lastHealthyBaseline.tests: Full Vitest suite passed: 170 files / 675 tests
lastHealthyBaseline.production: vcontrolhub-next and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws active; /api/status healthy; major unauth HTML/API probes returned 307/401/200 instead of 500; recent service logs clean

lastRun.summary: Performed the due fresh audit checkpoint after six successful remediation runs. No overlapping remediation/build/test process was found, git was clean before the audit, production services were active, /api/status was healthy, major protected pages and APIs returned expected 307/401/200 responses without 500s, recent service logs had no error/warn matches, and the full quality gate passed. No code defect requiring an immediate fix was found in this checkpoint; backlog/state were refreshed.
validation: pgrep overlap check found no previous remediation/build/test process other than the check itself
systemctl is-active vcontrolhub-next.service vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service returned active/active
HTTPS probes for /, /servers, /files, /downloads, /quick-services, /health, /monitoring, /deployments, /backups returned 307; /api/status 200; protected APIs 401
recent production service logs checked with no error/warn matches
lastRun.deployment: No code/runtime change was made in this audit checkpoint, so no production restart was required; current production services were verified active and smoke-tested successfully.
lastRun.nextTarget: Continue with the remaining P6 command/deployment background-task architecture hardening, or split out smaller deployable performance/availability slices discovered in the next backlog-driven pass.

runPolicy.batchSize: coherent deployable batch; may be larger for high-value architecture/performance/availability/user-experience improvements if fully verified
